name: Nid-Hca Mclennon
description_short: HUD-approved housing counseling agency providing free guidance on homeownership, financial stability, and affordable housing solutions through certified counselors.
description_long:
NID Housing Counseling Agency (NID-HCA) is a HUD-approved intermediary organization that operates as a dynamic network of advocacy groups, housing counselors, real estate professionals, community organizations, and faith-based groups. The agency functions as a multifaceted platform designed to empower individuals and communities through comprehensive housing solutions.

NID-HCA offers free housing counseling services delivered by HUD-certified counselors, homebuyer workshops, financial stability guidance, and personalized education on achieving homeownership. They provide advocacy support focused on improving access to quality housing and mortgage products, eliminating housing disparities, and connecting clients with down payment assistance programs. The agency also operates housing counseling courses and counselor training programs to equip professionals with skills for assisting clients in making informed housing decisions.

What distinguishes NID-HCA is their integration of advocacy, community education, and direct client services through a network model rather than a single provider. They document client success stories demonstrating tangible outcomes—such as their Atlanta client who secured $80,000 in down payment assistance to purchase a home, and another client who progressed from financial instability in May 2021 to homeownership by December 2023. They partner with HBCUs and MSIs to develop affordable housing solutions and maintain an existing client portal for secure document access.

However, the company profile lacks transparency on service scope, geographic coverage specifics, counselor availability timelines, and whether services extend beyond housing counseling to broader financial wellness. While they promise 48-hour response times to inquiries, specific service delivery standards and outcome metrics are not detailed. The website emphasizes their HUD-approved status but provides limited information about what specific housing products or programs they can help clients access.
pros: HUD-approved intermediary status ensures regulatory compliance and federal oversight
All services are completely free with no hidden fees or charges
Offers HUD-certified counselors for personalized one-on-one guidance
Documents real client success stories with specific outcomes (e.g., $80,000 down payment assistance)
Provides homebuyer workshops that qualify clients for down payment assistance programs
Operates as a network with partner organizations for broader community resource access
Remained operational during government shutdowns to serve clients in need
cons: Website lacks clear information on geographic service areas or which branches serve which regions
No specific details on average counselor response times or appointment availability beyond the 48-hour inquiry response promise
Limited transparency on the scope of services—unclear which housing products, loan programs, or lenders they can help clients access
No published counselor credentials, experience levels, or specializations listed
Website does not specify languages offered or accessibility accommodations for clients with disabilities
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking guidance on down payment assistance and mortgage readiness
Individuals working toward financial stability and building long-term homeownership goals
People experiencing housing instability or seeking to understand fair housing and mortgage options
Community members in underserved areas seeking to eliminate housing disparities through education
diagnosis: NID-HCA is best for individuals pursuing homeownership who need free expert guidance, down payment assistance navigation, and financial education from HUD-certified counselors. The primary caveat is that the website provides limited transparency on service geographic coverage, specific eligibility requirements, and which housing programs they can directly facilitate access to—prospective clients should contact them directly to confirm service availability in their location.
services: HUD-certified housing counseling through certified counselors
Homebuyer workshops and financial education courses
Financial stability counseling and goal-setting support
Down payment assistance program navigation and qualification support
Housing counseling training for professionals and counselors
Community workshops, webinars, and training sessions
Advocacy support on housing access and mortgage product issues
HBCU and MSI partnership programs for affordable student housing
Existing client secure portal for document access and case management
Community resource referrals through partner network
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1773 N. State Rd 7, Suite 200, Lauderhill, FL 33313
